column1: Do namespaces provide network isolation by default?
column2: No — any pod can reach any pod across namespaces by default; use NetworkPolicy for actual isolation

column1: Are object names unique cluster-wide or per-namespace?
column2: Per-namespace — two Deployments can both be named nginx if they're in different namespaces

column1: What are the four built-in Kubernetes namespaces?

column2: Node, PersistentVolume, ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding, Namespace itself, CustomResourceDefinition (any 3)

column1: What DNS suffix must you add to reach a Service from a different namespace?
column2: At minimum <service>.<namespace>; the full FQDN is <service>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local

column1: Are RBAC Roles namespace-scoped or cluster-scoped?
column2: Namespace-scoped — a Role/RoleBinding in one namespace grants no access elsewhere; use ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding for cluster-wide permissions

column1: What happens to all objects inside a namespace when you delete it?
column2: They are all deleted too — namespace deletion cascades completely, there is no selective delete

column1: A namespace is stuck in Terminating. What's the most likely cause?
column2: A finalizer on one of its resources hasn't cleared — check kubectl describe namespace <ns> for Conditions

column1: Does kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=<ns> change anything cluster-side?
column2: No — it's a client-side kubeconfig change only; it doesn't affect what other users' kubectl commands see

column1: A ResourceQuota sets pods: \0\" for a namespace. What happens when you create a Deployment there?"
column2: The Deployment object is created, but its ReplicaSet controller fails to create any Pods — the failure shows up in events, not the Deployment's own status line

column1: Which Kubernetes API group does the Namespace object belong to?
column2: The core v1 API group — not apps/v1

column1: What flag shows resources across every namespace at once?
